text: Wanna teach your kids English? Here's the best way. I don't have a child, but I do have a baby nephew
and my sister Amanda said that she'd be more than happy to teach you English expressions that you can use with your child.
My nephew Wes is the cutest baby ever. In this series, my sister will show her daily routine with Wes.
Since Wes is just starting to learn English too, maybe you can learn together.
Tonight is bath night, so we will get our sink tub. We put it in and we like to use a scrubber for his scalp to help get the shampoo in.
And we also use a filter for our water from the sink. We hang it up and then we turn our water on.
We have to make sure it's just right, not too cold, not too hot. Meanwhile, I'm going to go get his towel and washcloth.
Once we have his towel and his washcloth, we get his soap. We like to use pure cast steel soap because it has no scent or additives in it, so it's nice and moisturizing for the skin.
We pour just a tad. As you can hear, he's getting a little fuzzy waiting for his bath. We add the soap and then we add a little bit of water to make it fuzzy.
A really important thing about having a five-month-old is having a solid morning routine.
So, the first thing that we do is feeding, and right now he is breastfeeding. So to get everything ready for that, we like to get our bobby.
I always have my kindle nearby because breastfeeding takes a long time. He usually feeds from each side, and it takes about 40 minutes for him to completely get fed, full, and happy.
Good morning! Hi, baby! Good morning!
After we feed, we like to come out and do independent play while I cook breakfast. So we bring him out, and we put him in his play pan, which is very large and helps keep the dog out.
We let him play for a little bit while I cook, and then we get to play together.
We let him play for a little bit while I cook.
